key_requirements: Proposed: civil remedy for non-consensual deepfakes; no federal criminal statute enacted
notes: Multiple federal bills (DEFIANCE Act; NO FAKES Act; Protect Elections from Deceptive AI Act) introduced; none enacted as of early 2026. FTC Act §5 provides limited basis for deceptive deepfake enforcement.

key_requirements: Non-consensual deepfake intimate imagery prohibited; political deepfake disclosure required within 60 days of election; civil right of action for victims
notes: Most active U.S. state regulator. Does not address commercial fraud, authentication bypass, or AI watermarking.

key_requirements: Prohibition on deepfakes depicting sexual conduct without consent; criminal penalties up to 2 years
notes: Narrowest scope of any reviewed jurisdiction. Criminal statute only; no civil remedy; no political deepfake provisions.

key_requirements: Mandatory labeling of AI-generated content (Art. 50); watermarking of GPAI synthetic media; platform distribution obligations; GPAI systemic risk enhanced requirements
enforcement_body: EU AI Office + national competent authorities
notes: Most comprehensive binding framework. GPAI obligations in force August 2025; full high-risk obligations from August 2026. Enforcement against non-EU informal-channel providers remains practically difficult.

primary_legislation: CAC Deep Synthesis Regs (2022) + AIGC Measures (2023)
key_requirements: Mandatory content labeling; platform liability; real-name registration for providers; prohibited uses include political manipulation and fraud
notes: Most assertive national framework on paper. Enforcement applied inconsistently, particularly in politically sensitive contexts per international observers. Limited enforcement transparency.

primary_legislation: Online Safety Act 2023 + Criminal Justice Bill 2024
key_requirements: Illegal content platform duties (CSAM deepfakes; fraud-enabling content); deepfake intimate imagery sharing criminalized (up to 2 years)
notes: Strong platform accountability via Online Safety Act. No general AI content transparency or watermarking requirement equivalent to EU AI Act.

key_requirements: Existing criminal law applied to some deepfake misuse; eSafety Commissioner has limited harmful content jurisdiction; no deepfake-specific transparency or watermarking requirements
notes: Australian Law Reform Commission reviewing deepfake harms as of 2026. No comprehensive framework enacted. Regulatory gap acknowledged by government.
